question: How strongly does cumulative engagement relate to final grade overall?
verdict: Moderate, robust positive association across all five cohorts.

question: Is the association heterogeneous, and does delivery mode explain it?
verdict: Low-moderate heterogeneity; delivery condition is NOT a significant moderator.

question: Does engagement matter more for weaker students? (week 11)
statistic: Mean (IDF slope at tau=0.1 minus tau=0.5), grade pts
verdict: Yes - effect is markedly larger in the lower grade tail (early-warning value).

question: Is the lower-tail excess formally supported? (bootstrap CI)
verdict: Lower-tail excess present descriptively; bootstrap support varies by cohort.

question: Which indicator has the strongest lower-tail association? (univariate QR)
statistic: Largest mean tau=0.1 slope across cohorts (week 11)
verdict: Univariate quantile regression identifies which component drives tail effects.

question: Are conclusions sensitive to model specification (OLS vs fractional/beta)?
statistic: Sign agreement of Diversity coef across 3 model families

question: Which engagement dimension contributes most? (week 11 LMG)
statistic: Mean LMG share: top vs bottom indicator
verdict: Diversity/Immediacy carry more unique signal than raw Frequency.

question: Is the LMG ranking stable at mid-term? (week 6 vs 11)
statistic: Top indicator at week 6 vs week 11
verdict: Same top indicator at mid-term and end-of-term.

question: Does engagement track individual/exam work more than group/peer work?
verdict: Engagement aligns significantly more with exam than peer-marked components.

question: How early does the engagement signal stabilise?
verdict: Stabilises early when effect is flat; accrues to wk~9 where signal grows (STAT0002 in-person).

question: Do timing/regularity features add value beyond F/I/D?
statistic: Cohorts with significant incremental R2 (wk6) / mean dR2
verdict: Yes - regularity/timing add significant explanatory power (esp. STAT0002).

question: Do latent engagement trajectories differentiate outcomes?
statistic: Kruskal-Wallis grade ~ class; grade gap (top-bottom class)
verdict: Distinct trajectory classes; large grade gap; composition stable across conditions.

question: Does the association survive programme adjustment and multiple testing?
statistic: Max slope attenuation; BH-significant weekly tests
verdict: Association robust to degree-programme control and family-wise correction.
